that's ok.

That's interesting.

which we'll get some people's attention because the main reason why people don't move is because it's so darn expensive, of course.

Yeah.
Ok.
So that's
hopefully that works.

I, I see about half and half some places almost never do.
Kind of like how we were operating.
They didn't really see the need in it.

Fair enough.
Fair enough.

Is it coming through?

So that's been really interesting for some folks who've, who've explored it.
I turned it on real quick so that we can see what it looks like in your, in your platform.
Um But just some of the kind of the improvements that we are making here to try and scratch as many of the itches as we can from like a quality of life perspective when using apartment IQ.
And then we have a couple of things coming.
Um We are in the final stages of our closed beta with the market survey tab.
So rather than your traditional apartment IQ user experience, there's actually gonna be a new tab here at the top and it's going to look a lot more like a traditional market survey.
It's gonna have your columns and look a lot more um like those traditional experiences that you probably had in the past when you were doing manual surveys even.
So our hope there is that it becomes a lot more of a familiar tool, especially for property and community managers to use.
Um And, and that it kind of drives a few more insights that way.
So that's coming.
Uh And then with that will be an amenities, fees and deposits uh as well as a new way for us to calculate occupancy
and that'll be coming towards the end of Q four.
We're pretty
a new way to, I'm sorry, a new way to calculate occupancy.
What do you mean?
Yes.
So right now we have had a advertised occupancy for a very long time.

Uh The problem with advertised occupancy of course is that we can only uh or rather that some properties hide certain units, some properties will leave a unit on perpetually as open kind of that loss leader unit to attract traffic.
Um So this can be a little bit of a funny number to highlight.
Um So it is consistent, right?
Because we are delivering to you every available unit on a property website against what we know their total units to be.
However, a lot of the feedback we have heard sometimes is that it's there, there's too much of a difference between this number and the number that you hear when you call a property directly.
So we need to actually close that gap and get to within 5 to 10% of actual occupancies with the new algorithm that we're developing now.
OK.
So that's what it, yeah, so it's uh hopefully gonna be a lot closer to the target.
OK, perfect.

And with some of those additional calculations, particularly as part of the market survey will be things like leasing percentages and uh rolling leasing numbers over seven days over 30 days so that you get a better understanding of what velocity could be without having to do any kind of math.
When you look at perhaps um the new versus leased graph or perhaps when you're taking a look at um vacant units against availability and then recently leased.
